description: deploy an NVIDIA VSS compose profile: probe credentials/remote endpoints, resolve + normalize compose, then docker compose up -d
rules: destructive: brings up the VSS stack with docker compose, pulls NIM images, mutates host infrastructure
always start from a fresh copy of the source .env (generated.env); never mutate the checked-in .env
never skip the dry-run (docker compose config > resolved.yml)
MUST run normalize_resolved_yml.py after config and before up -d to drop dangling optional depends_on
--env-file generated.env is mandatory on up -d or COMPOSE_PROFILES may be unset and up -d exits 0 with zero services
credential probes (check_credentials.sh / probe_remote_models.sh) are read-only and run before any env mutation
usage: Set REPO (the video-search-and-summarization checkout) and PROFILE (base|alerts|lvs|search|warehouse|edge). Output: deploy.json.
limitation: Deploys compose-based VSS profiles only; standalone microservice deployment belongs to the matching vss-deploy-* skill. Hardware sizing, model placement, and profile-specific readiness are owned by profile references — do not infer from memory. Privileged host remediation requires user approval when passwordless sudo is unavailable.
